description: Phase 30: Mobile Parity - Capacitor mega-shell project with native plugins, splash/icons, OAuth, deep linking, widgets, offline mode, crash reporting, and app size budget enforcement
steps: 1. Create `apps/quant-mobile/` as the mega-shell Capacitor project. Create package.json with @capacitor/core, @capacitor/cli, @capacitor/android, @capacitor/ios, and all native plugin deps. Include capacitor.config.ts with appId 'com.quant.app', appName 'Quant', webDir 'dist'. Create tsconfig.json and vitest.config.ts.

2. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/plugins/` directory. Create native plugin TypeScript wrapper files: push-notifications.ts (FCM + APNs abstraction), contacts.ts, camera-media.ts, file-system.ts, share-extension.ts, biometric-auth.ts, background-fetch.ts, webrtc.ts, haptics.ts, in-app-browser.ts. Each exports a typed service class wrapping Capacitor plugin APIs.

3. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/splash-icons/` with configuration types: SplashConfig (backgroundColor, logo, fadeTime, autoHide), AppIconSet (adaptive icon config for Android, AppIcon catalog for iOS), AdaptiveIconConfig (foreground, background, monochrome layers).

4. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/auth/` with oauth.ts: NativeOAuthService class supporting Apple Sign-In (ASAuthorizationController wrapper) and Google Sign-In (Google Identity SDK wrapper). Methods: signInWithApple(), signInWithGoogle(), handleRedirect(), getTokens(). Uses PKCE flow.

5. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/deep-linking/` with deep-link-handler.ts: UniversalLinkHandler class. Methods: registerRoutes(routeMap), handleIncomingUrl(url), generateAppLink(route, params). Supports iOS universal links (apple-app-site-association) and Android App Links (assetlinks.json). Include config types for both platforms.

6. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/widgets/` with widget-bridge.ts: WidgetBridge class that syncs widget data from the main app. Types: NativeWidget (iOS WidgetKit + Android Glance), WidgetTimeline, WidgetEntry. Methods: updateWidget(id, data), registerWidget(config), scheduleRefresh(interval).

7. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/offline/` with offline-sync.ts: MobileOfflineSync class. Integrates with @quant/sync-engine's OfflineOperationQueue and SyncProtocol. Methods: queueMutation(operation), replayOnReconnect(), getQueueStatus(), clearQueue(). Handles network state transitions (online/offline events).

8. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/crash-reporting/` with crash-reporter.ts: CrashReporter class (Sentry/GlitchTip compatible). Methods: init(dsn, config), captureException(error, context), captureMessage(message, level), setUser(user), addBreadcrumb(breadcrumb). Respects privacy - no PII in crash reports unless user opts in.

9. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/performance/` with perf-budget.ts: AppSizeBudget class. Methods: checkBundleSize(buildOutput) -> passes if < 30MB, measureColdStart() -> target < 2s, auditAssets(dir) -> identifies oversized assets. Types: PerformanceReport, SizeBudgetConfig.

10. Create `apps/quant-mobile/src/app-launcher.ts`: AppLauncher class - the mega-shell's in-app launcher that shows all 13 Quant apps. Methods: getInstalledApps(), launchApp(appId), getAppStatus(appId). Uses a tab/grid UI to switch between Quant apps within the single shell.
11. Create barrel export at `apps/quant-mobile/src/index.ts` exporting all plugin wrappers, services, and types.

12. Write tests in `apps/quant-mobile/src/__tests__/`: push-notifications.test.ts, oauth.test.ts, deep-link-handler.test.ts, offline-sync.test.ts, crash-reporter.test.ts, perf-budget.test.ts, app-launcher.test.ts. Test the logic, mock native APIs.
acceptance_criteria: apps/quant-mobile typechecks and builds without errors
All native plugin wrappers export typed interfaces matching Capacitor plugin APIs
OAuth service supports both Apple Sign-In and Google Sign-In with PKCE
Deep linking handles both iOS universal links and Android App Links
Crash reporter is privacy-respecting (no PII by default)
App size budget enforcer checks < 30MB threshold
